title: From Evaluation to Guardrails: What We Brought to ACM FAccT 2026
excerpt: At ACM FAccT, we demonstrated why AI guardrails need the same scrutiny as models. Moving from static policies to context- and language-specific evaluations, our hands-on session proved that agentic guardrails equipped with tools like web search are vital for reliable, real-world deployment.
content_html:
Technical Content

At ACM FAccT, we demonstrated why AI guardrails need the same scrutiny as models. Moving from static policies to context- and language-specific evaluations, our hands-on session proved that agentic guardrails equipped with tools like web search are vital for reliable, real-world deployment.

In June, we traveled to Montreal for the ACM Conference on Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency (ACM FAccT), the premier venue for safe and responsible AI development; a conference where computer scientists, social scientists, policymakers, and lawyers share a room to ask not just how to build AI systems, but whether, when, and for whom. It was the right audience for our tutorial, Contextual Evaluation of LLM Guardrails Across Languages and Agentic Systems.

Evaluation is the throughline

Evaluation has been one of the main conversations in AI safety and a key step toward responsible deployment. The field is shifting from celebrating general capabilities to measuring real-world, domain- and language-specific performance, and cross-sector efforts like the EvalEval Coalition are now building the science and infrastructure for evaluating evaluations themselves. Despite the money spent on evals, benchmark saturation, and evaluation data going stale, one practical function endures: evaluation shapes guardrails. When an eval shows a model produces harmful or toxic content in a given context or language, one response is to design a guardrail around that exact failure.

Guardrails deserve the same scrutiny as models

Guardrails – the mechanisms that filter, flag, or constrain LLM inputs and outputs – shape what users actually see in chatbots, platforms, and public-facing services. Yet they get far less scrutiny than the models they govern. Historically, they were proprietary classifiers, visible only through unexplained refusals. Open-source guardrail models and policy-prompt guardrails makes independent evaluation possible.

Our argument at FAccT was simple: evaluating guardrails is as important as evaluating the LLMs they protect, and context- and language-specific evaluation results should inform guardrails that move beyond static taxonomies of harm toward dynamic policies. 

The path from evaluation to guardrail

That path is what brought us to FAccT, because connecting the two is rarely straightforward. Our route: a community and language-informed evaluation of 120 refugee and asylum-focused scenario pairs across English, Farsi, Arabic, Kurdish-Sorani, and Pashto, scored by native-speaker evaluators from Respond Crisis Translation on six rights-based criteria. We published the results as the open MHRE evaluation data on Mozilla Data Collective, on terms set with the evaluators, and turned the recurring failures such as unsafe referrals, missing disclaimers, and stereotyped assumptions into concrete guardrail policies in English and Farsi.

Testing those policies exposed a structural gap: criteria like factuality and actionability cannot be judged from text alone. Does this NGO exist? Is this law current in this jurisdiction? Often text-only guardrails rubber-stamped responses they had no way to verify, hallucinated terms, and scored identical English and Farsi policies differently (check out our blogpost). So we formed a hypothesis: LLM-enabled guardrails need tools such as search, retrieval, and fact-checking to judge in a more reliable and trustworthy manner. That was the agentic guardrail we brought to Montreal to test.

What the hands-on session showed

The overall methodology and the case  for contextual guardrails, with language- and context-dependent policies and tools, resonated with participants. Thirty-five participants ran our demo by selecting their own scenarios and policies and comparing agentic and non-agentic judges on the same responses.

Tools changed the supporting evidence more often than the final verdict: 90% of verdicts agreed across both modes. However, “agentic” behavior depended heavily on the underlying judge LLM. Claude Sonnet 4.6 used web search on every run (4.1 tool calls per run on average), whereas GPT-5 Nano rarely did (0.2 tool calls per run). When tools were invoked, they influenced outcomes in both directions: verifying the factual claims in an asylum-related response increased its score, while identifying a factual error that the tool-less judge had overlooked downgraded the verdict from pass to borderline.

Tooling that makes this practical

None of this experimentation is feasible if every comparison requires new engineering. Mozilla AI’s open-source any-guardrail gives a unified interface for choosing and swapping guardrails with custom policies, making the guardrail layer as configurable as the model. And Otari, Mozilla’s new open-source LLM gateway, lets you choose and switch the LLMs behind your judges seamlessly.

What’s next

We are continuing to refine our design and test whether tool access makes LLM-enabled guardrails more reliable and trustworthy across humanitarian, financial, and social-engineering use cases, with a wider array of tools and contextual scenarios, in English <-> Farsi and English <->Spanish. Results are coming; stay tuned, and hopefully see you at the next ACM FAccT!

Disclaimer on LLM use: Roya Pakzad used Claude Opus 4.8 to copyedit this post.

blocks.html_en: At ACM FAccT, we demonstrated why AI guardrails need the same scrutiny as models. Moving from static policies to context- and language-specific evaluations, our hands-on session proved that agentic guardrails equipped with tools like web search are vital for reliable, real-world deployment.

sentences.en: At ACM FAccT, we demonstrated why AI guardrails need the same scrutiny as models.
sentences.zh: 在 ACM FAccT 上，我们展示了为什么人工智能护栏需要与模型一样的审查。

sentences.en: Moving from static policies to context- and language-specific evaluations, our hands-on session proved that agentic guardrails equipped with tools like web search are vital for reliable, real-world deployment.
sentences.zh: 从静态策略到特定于上下文和语言的评估，我们的实践课程证明，配备网络搜索等工具的代理护栏对于可靠的现实部署至关重要。

blocks.html_en: In June, we traveled to Montreal for the ACM Conference on Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency (ACM FAccT), the premier venue for safe and responsible AI development; a conference where computer scientists, social scientists, policymakers, and lawyers share a room to ask not just how to build AI systems, but whether, when, and for whom. It was the right audience for our tutorial, Contextual Evaluation of LLM Guardrails Across Languages and Agentic Systems.

sentences.en: In June, we traveled to Montreal for the ACM Conference on Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency (ACM FAccT), the premier venue for safe and responsible AI development; a conference where computer scientists, social scientists, policymakers, and lawyers share a room to ask not just how to build AI systems, but whether, when, and for whom.
sentences.zh: 6 月，我们前往蒙特利尔参加 ACM 公平、问责和透明度会议 (ACM FAccT)，这是安全和负责任的人工智能开发的首要场所；这是一场计算机科学家、社会科学家、政策制定者和律师共处一室的会议，不仅要问如何构建人工智能系统，还要问是否、何时以及为谁构建人工智能系统。

sentences.en: It was the right audience for our tutorial, Contextual Evaluation of LLM Guardrails Across Languages and Agentic Systems.
sentences.zh: 这是我们的教程“跨语言和代理系统的 LLM Guardrails 的上下文评估”的正确受众。

sentences.en: Evaluation is the throughline
sentences.zh: 评价是贯穿的

The field is shifting from celebrating general capabilities to measuring real-world, domain- and language-specific performance, and cross-sector efforts like the EvalEval Coalition are now building the science and infrastructure for evaluating evaluations themselves. Despite the money spent on evals, benchmark saturation, and evaluation data going stale, one practical function endures: evaluation shapes guardrails. When an eval shows a model produces harmful or toxic content in a given context or language, one response is to design a guardrail around that exact failure.

sentences.en: Evaluation has been one of the main conversations in AI safety and a key step toward responsible deployment.
sentences.zh: 评估一直是人工智能安全的主要话题之一，也是实现负责任部署的关键一步。

sentences.en: The field is shifting from celebrating general capabilities to measuring real-world, domain- and language-specific performance, and cross-sector efforts like the EvalEval Coalition are now building the science and infrastructure for evaluating evaluations themselves.
sentences.zh: 该领域正在从庆祝一般能力转向衡量现实世界、特定领域和特定语言的性能，EvalEval 联盟等跨部门的努力现在正在构建用于评估评估本身的科学和基础设施。

sentences.en: Despite the money spent on evals, benchmark saturation, and evaluation data going stale, one practical function endures: evaluation shapes guardrails.
sentences.zh: 尽管在评估、基准饱和和评估数据上花费了大量资金，但一项实用功能仍然存在：评估塑造护栏。

sentences.en: When an eval shows a model produces harmful or toxic content in a given context or language, one response is to design a guardrail around that exact failure.
sentences.zh: 当评估显示模型在给定上下文或语言中产生有害或有毒内容时，一种应对措施是围绕该确切故障设计一个护栏。

blocks.html_en: Guardrails deserve the same scrutiny as models

sentences.en: Guardrails deserve the same scrutiny as models
sentences.zh: 护栏值得与模型同样的审查

blocks.html_en: Guardrails – the mechanisms that filter, flag, or constrain LLM inputs and outputs – shape what users actually see in chatbots, platforms, and public-facing services. Yet they get far less scrutiny than the models they govern. Historically, they were proprietary classifiers, visible only through unexplained refusals. Open-source guardrail models and policy-prompt guardrails makes independent evaluation possible.

sentences.en: Guardrails – the mechanisms that filter, flag, or constrain LLM inputs and outputs – shape what users actually see in chatbots, platforms, and public-facing services.
sentences.zh: 护栏——过滤、标记或限制法学硕士输入和输出的机制——塑造用户在聊天机器人、平台和面向公众的服务中实际看到的内容。

sentences.en: Yet they get far less scrutiny than the models they govern.
sentences.zh: 然而，与他们所管理的模式相比，他们受到的审查要少得多。

sentences.en: Historically, they were proprietary classifiers, visible only through unexplained refusals.
sentences.zh: 从历史上看，它们是专有的分类器，只有通过不明原因的拒绝才能看到。

sentences.en: Open-source guardrail models and policy-prompt guardrails makes independent evaluation possible.
sentences.zh: 开源护栏模型和政策提示护栏使独立评估成为可能。

blocks.html_en: Our argument at FAccT was simple: evaluating guardrails is as important as evaluating the LLMs they protect, and context- and language-specific evaluation results should inform guardrails that move beyond static taxonomies of harm toward dynamic policies.

sentences.en: Our argument at FAccT was simple: evaluating guardrails is as important as evaluating the LLMs they protect, and context- and language-specific evaluation results should inform guardrails that move beyond static taxonomies of harm toward dynamic policies.
sentences.zh: 我们在 FAccT 上的论点很简单：评估护栏与评估它们所保护的法学硕士同样重要，并且特定于上下文和语言的评估结果应该为护栏提供信息，从而超越静态伤害分类法，转向动态政策。

sentences.en: The path from evaluation to guardrail
sentences.zh: 从评估到护栏的路径

blocks.html_en: That path is what brought us to FAccT, because connecting the two is rarely straightforward. Our route: a community and language-informed evaluation of 120 refugee and asylum-focused scenario pairs across English, Farsi, Arabic, Kurdish-Sorani, and Pashto, scored by native-speaker evaluators from Respond Crisis Translation on six rights-based criteria. We published the results as the open MHRE evaluation data on Mozilla Data Collective, on terms set with the evaluators, and turned the recurring failures such as unsafe referrals, missing disclaimers, and stereotyped assumptions into concrete guardrail policies in English and Farsi.

sentences.en: That path is what brought us to FAccT, because connecting the two is rarely straightforward.
sentences.zh: 正是这条道路将我们带到了 FAccT，因为将两者联系起来很少是直截了当的。

sentences.en: Our route: a community and language-informed evaluation of 120 refugee and asylum-focused scenario pairs across English, Farsi, Arabic, Kurdish-Sorani, and Pashto, scored by native-speaker evaluators from Respond Crisis Translation on six rights-based criteria.
sentences.zh: 我们的路线：对英语、波斯语、阿拉伯语、库尔德语-索拉尼语和普什图语中的 120 个以难民和庇护为重点的情景对进行社区和语言评估，由响应危机翻译的母语评估员根据六项基于权利的标准进行评分。

sentences.en: We published the results as the open MHRE evaluation data on Mozilla Data Collective, on terms set with the evaluators, and turned the recurring failures such as unsafe referrals, missing disclaimers, and stereotyped assumptions into concrete guardrail policies in English and Farsi.
sentences.zh: 我们按照与评估者设定的条款，将结果作为开放的 MHRE 评估数据发布在 Mozilla Data Collective 上，并将反复出现的失败（例如不安全的推荐、缺失免责声明和刻板的假设）转化为英语和波斯语的具体护栏政策。

blocks.html_en: Testing those policies exposed a structural gap: criteria like factuality and actionability cannot be judged from text alone. Does this NGO exist? Is this law current in this jurisdiction? Often text-only guardrails rubber-stamped responses they had no way to verify, hallucinated terms, and scored identical English and Farsi policies differently (check out our blogpost). So we formed a hypothesis: LLM-enabled guardrails need tools such as search, retrieval, and fact-checking to judge in a more reliable and trustworthy manner. That was the agentic guardrail we brought to Montreal to test.

sentences.en: Testing those policies exposed a structural gap: criteria like factuality and actionability cannot be judged from text alone.
sentences.zh: 测试这些政策暴露了结构性差距：事实性和可操作性等标准不能仅从文本来判断。

sentences.en: Does this NGO exist?
sentences.zh: 这个非政府组织存在吗？

sentences.en: Is this law current in this jurisdiction?
sentences.zh: 该法律在该司法管辖区有效吗？

sentences.en: Often text-only guardrails rubber-stamped responses they had no way to verify, hallucinated terms, and scored identical English and Farsi policies differently (check out our blogpost).
sentences.zh: 通常，纯文本的护栏是橡皮图章式的反应，他们无法验证，产生幻觉术语，并对相同的英语和波斯语政策进行不同的评分（查看我们的博客文章）。

sentences.en: So we formed a hypothesis: LLM-enabled guardrails need tools such as search, retrieval, and fact-checking to judge in a more reliable and trustworthy manner.
sentences.zh: 因此我们形成了一个假设：支持LLM的护栏需要搜索、检索和事实核查等工具来以更可靠和值得信赖的方式进行判断。

sentences.en: That was the agentic guardrail we brought to Montreal to test.
sentences.zh: 这就是我们带到蒙特利尔测试的代理护栏。

sentences.en: What the hands-on session showed
sentences.zh: 实践课程展示了什么

blocks.html_en: The overall methodology and the case  for contextual guardrails, with language- and context-dependent policies and tools, resonated with participants. Thirty-five participants ran our demo by selecting their own scenarios and policies and comparing agentic and non-agentic judges on the same responses.

sentences.en: The overall methodology and the case for contextual guardrails, with language- and context-dependent policies and tools, resonated with participants.
sentences.zh: 上下文护栏的总体方法和案例，以及与语言和上下文相关的政策和工具，引起了参与者的共鸣。

sentences.en: Thirty-five participants ran our demo by selecting their own scenarios and policies and comparing agentic and non-agentic judges on the same responses.
sentences.zh: 三十五名参与者通过选择自己的场景和策略并比较代理和非代理法官对相同答案的方式来运行我们的演示。

blocks.html_en: Tools changed the supporting evidence more often than the final verdict: 90% of verdicts agreed across both modes. However, “agentic” behavior depended heavily on the underlying judge LLM. Claude Sonnet 4.6 used web search on every run (4.1 tool calls per run on average), whereas GPT-5 Nano rarely did (0.2 tool calls per run). When tools were invoked, they influenced outcomes in both directions: verifying the factual claims in an asylum-related response increased its score, while identifying a factual error that the tool-less judge had overlooked downgraded the verdict from pass to borderline.

sentences.en: Tools changed the supporting evidence more often than the final verdict: 90% of verdicts agreed across both modes.
sentences.zh: 工具改变支持证据的次数比最终判决的次数还要多：两种模式下 90% 的判决都达成一致。

sentences.en: However, “agentic” behavior depended heavily on the underlying judge LLM.
sentences.zh: 然而，“代理”行为在很大程度上取决于底层法官的法学硕士学位。

sentences.en: Claude Sonnet 4.6 used web search on every run (4.1 tool calls per run on average), whereas GPT-5 Nano rarely did (0.2 tool calls per run).
sentences.zh: Claude Sonnet 4.6 在每次运行时都使用网络搜索（平均每次运行 4.1 次工具调用），而 GPT-5 Nano 很少这样做（每次运行 0.2 次工具调用）。

sentences.en: When tools were invoked, they influenced outcomes in both directions: verifying the factual claims in an asylum-related response increased its score, while identifying a factual error that the tool-less judge had overlooked downgraded the verdict from pass to borderline.
sentences.zh: 当工具被调用时，它们会在两个方向上影响结果：验证与庇护相关的答复中的事实主张提高了分数，同时发现了无工具法官忽视的事实错误，将判决从通过降到了边缘。

sentences.en: Tooling that makes this practical
sentences.zh: 使这变得实用的工具

blocks.html_en: None of this experimentation is feasible if every comparison requires new engineering. Mozilla AI’s open-source any-guardrail gives a unified interface for choosing and swapping guardrails with custom policies, making the guardrail layer as configurable as the model. And Otari, Mozilla’s new open-source LLM gateway, lets you choose and switch the LLMs behind your judges seamlessly.

sentences.en: None of this experimentation is feasible if every comparison requires new engineering.
sentences.zh: 如果每次比较都需要新的工程，那么这些实验都是不可行的。

sentences.en: Mozilla AI’s open-source any-guardrail gives a unified interface for choosing and swapping guardrails with custom policies, making the guardrail layer as configurable as the model.
sentences.zh: Mozilla AI 的开源 any-guardrail 提供了一个统一的界面，用于选择和交换具有自定义策略的 Guardrail，使 Guardrail 层与模型一样可配置。

sentences.en: And Otari, Mozilla’s new open-source LLM gateway, lets you choose and switch the LLMs behind your judges seamlessly.
sentences.zh: Mozilla 的新开源 LLM 网关 Otari 可以让您无缝地选择和切换评委身后的 LLM。

sentences.en: What’s next
sentences.zh: 接下来是什么

blocks.html_en: We are continuing to refine our design and test whether tool access makes LLM-enabled guardrails more reliable and trustworthy across humanitarian, financial, and social-engineering use cases, with a wider array of tools and contextual scenarios, in English <-> Farsi and English <->Spanish. Results are coming; stay tuned, and hopefully see you at the next ACM FAccT!

sentences.en: We are continuing to refine our design and test whether tool access makes LLM-enabled guardrails more reliable and trustworthy across humanitarian, financial, and social-engineering use cases, with a wider array of tools and contextual scenarios, in English <-> Farsi and English <->Spanish.
sentences.zh: 我们正在继续完善我们的设计，并测试工具访问是否使支持 LLM 的护栏在人道主义、金融和社会工程用例中更加可靠和值得信赖，并提供更广泛的工具和上下文场景，语言为英语 <-> 波斯语和英语 <-> 西班牙语。

sentences.en: Results are coming; stay tuned, and hopefully see you at the next ACM FAccT!
sentences.zh: 结果即将到来；请继续关注，希望在下一届 ACM FaccT 上见到您！

sentences.en: Disclaimer on LLM use: Roya Pakzad used Claude Opus 4.8 to copyedit this post.
sentences.zh: LLM 使用免责声明：Roya Pakzad 使用 Claude Opus 4.8 来复制编辑这篇文章。

content_html_zh:
Technical Content

在 ACM FAccT 上，我们展示了为什么人工智能护栏需要与模型一样的审查。 从静态策略到特定于上下文和语言的评估，我们的实践课程证明，配备网络搜索等工具的代理护栏对于可靠的现实部署至关重要。

6 月，我们前往蒙特利尔参加 ACM 公平、问责和透明度会议 (ACM FAccT)，这是安全和负责任的人工智能开发的首要场所；这是一场计算机科学家、社会科学家、政策制定者和律师共处一室的会议，不仅要问如何构建人工智能系统，还要问是否、何时以及为谁构建人工智能系统。 这是我们的教程“跨语言和代理系统的 LLM Guardrails 的上下文评估”的正确受众。

评价是贯穿的

评估一直是人工智能安全的主要话题之一，也是实现负责任部署的关键一步。 该领域正在从庆祝一般能力转向衡量现实世界、特定领域和特定语言的性能，EvalEval 联盟等跨部门的努力现在正在构建用于评估评估本身的科学和基础设施。 尽管在评估、基准饱和和评估数据上花费了大量资金，但一项实用功能仍然存在：评估塑造护栏。 当评估显示模型在给定上下文或语言中产生有害或有毒内容时，一种应对措施是围绕该确切故障设计一个护栏。

护栏值得与模型同样的审查

护栏——过滤、标记或限制法学硕士输入和输出的机制——塑造用户在聊天机器人、平台和面向公众的服务中实际看到的内容。 然而，与他们所管理的模式相比，他们受到的审查要少得多。 从历史上看，它们是专有的分类器，只有通过不明原因的拒绝才能看到。 开源护栏模型和政策提示护栏使独立评估成为可能。

我们在 FAccT 上的论点很简单：评估护栏与评估它们所保护的法学硕士同样重要，并且特定于上下文和语言的评估结果应该为护栏提供信息，从而超越静态伤害分类法，转向动态政策。

从评估到护栏的路径

正是这条道路将我们带到了 FAccT，因为将两者联系起来很少是直截了当的。 我们的路线：对英语、波斯语、阿拉伯语、库尔德语-索拉尼语和普什图语中的 120 个以难民和庇护为重点的情景对进行社区和语言评估，由响应危机翻译的母语评估员根据六项基于权利的标准进行评分。 我们按照与评估者设定的条款，将结果作为开放的 MHRE 评估数据发布在 Mozilla Data Collective 上，并将反复出现的失败（例如不安全的推荐、缺失免责声明和刻板的假设）转化为英语和波斯语的具体护栏政策。

测试这些政策暴露了结构性差距：事实性和可操作性等标准不能仅从文本来判断。 这个非政府组织存在吗？ 该法律在该司法管辖区有效吗？ 通常，纯文本的护栏是橡皮图章式的反应，他们无法验证，产生幻觉术语，并对相同的英语和波斯语政策进行不同的评分（查看我们的博客文章）。 因此我们形成了一个假设：支持LLM的护栏需要搜索、检索和事实核查等工具来以更可靠和值得信赖的方式进行判断。 这就是我们带到蒙特利尔测试的代理护栏。

实践课程展示了什么

上下文护栏的总体方法和案例，以及与语言和上下文相关的政策和工具，引起了参与者的共鸣。 三十五名参与者通过选择自己的场景和策略并比较代理和非代理法官对相同答案的方式来运行我们的演示。

工具改变支持证据的次数比最终判决的次数还要多：两种模式下 90% 的判决都达成一致。 然而，“代理”行为在很大程度上取决于底层法官的法学硕士学位。 Claude Sonnet 4.6 在每次运行时都使用网络搜索（平均每次运行 4.1 次工具调用），而 GPT-5 Nano 很少这样做（每次运行 0.2 次工具调用）。 当工具被调用时，它们会在两个方向上影响结果：验证与庇护相关的答复中的事实主张提高了分数，同时发现了无工具法官忽视的事实错误，将判决从通过降到了边缘。

使这变得实用的工具

如果每次比较都需要新的工程，那么这些实验都是不可行的。 Mozilla AI 的开源 any-guardrail 提供了一个统一的界面，用于选择和交换具有自定义策略的 Guardrail，使 Guardrail 层与模型一样可配置。 Mozilla 的新开源 LLM 网关 Otari 可以让您无缝地选择和切换评委身后的 LLM。

接下来是什么

我们正在继续完善我们的设计，并测试工具访问是否使支持 LLM 的护栏在人道主义、金融和社会工程用例中更加可靠和值得信赖，并提供更广泛的工具和上下文场景，语言为英语 <-> 波斯语和英语 <-> 西班牙语。 结果即将到来；请继续关注，希望在下一届 ACM FaccT 上见到您！
